key_insight: HCC-STAR is a clinically aligned LLM that turns routine HCC EMR text into risk staging, guideline-consistent treatment rankings, and individualized survival estimates.
clinical_relevance: It may help clinicians stratify hepatocellular carcinoma more precisely and support faster, more evidence-based treatment decisions, but it is a decision-support tool rather than a substitute for clinical judgment.
method: Curated ~30,000 HCC cases from SEER and expanded them into EMR-style narratives with clinician-validated prompt augmentation.
Built a knowledge-aligned reasoning framework optimized with a step-verifiable composite reward.
Evaluated the model on a multi-center cohort of 6,668 patients from 12 hospitals in China against guidelines and competitive LLMs.
evidence_signal: In multi-center testing, HCC-STAR outperformed clinical guidelines and models such as GPT-5 and Gemini-2.5 Pro on treatment recommendation and risk stratification, with blinded specialists rating its reasoning as trustworthy.
watch_item: Performance is based on retrospective and hypothetical analyses, so external prospective validation and careful integration into real-world workflows are still needed.
paper_title: Towards Precision Therapy in Hepatocellular Carcinoma: A Clinical-Reasoning LLM for Risk Stratification and Treatment Guidance
